item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents move toward balance without a full reversal. Agent A starts clearly pro-society and later repeatedly adds that individual rights must be protected, while still saying Indonesian collectivism and social justice often put social interests above individual rights. Agent B remains rights-first throughout but moves from stronger individual-rights priority toward acknowledging that social policy remains relevant if it does not violate basic personal freedom.
